cases.eval_name: Upload a video file to YouTube

assertions.text: Checks that ~/Desktop/tutorial.mp4 exists before attempting upload
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output only lists file verification as a planned step ('Verify the file — confirm ~/Desktop/tutorial.mp4 exists') but does not actually perform the check. It is requesting permission to run Bash commands rather than having executed the existence check.

assertions.text: Includes AppleScript or shell command to handle the file picker dialog with the correct path
assertions.reasoning: The output only describes the plan to use AppleScript but does not include any actual AppleScript or shell commands to handle the file picker dialog with the correct path.

assertions.text: Mentions setting video title/description or asks the user to provide them
assertions.reasoning: The LLM output focuses entirely on technical setup steps (Chrome profile discovery, file verification, launching Chrome, AppleScript automation) and permission requirements. It does not mention setting a video title or description, nor does it ask the user to provide them.

actionItems.summary: The skill actively degrades output: baseline scores 4.5/5 vs skill's 3.0/5, with 0% assertion pass rate. The skill causes the LLM to narrate plans and request permissions instead of executing actions directly.
weaknesses: Skill induces permission-seeking behavior: instead of running Bash commands to check file existence, the LLM only lists 'Verify the file' as a planned step
Skill fails to produce actual AppleScript code in the response — only describes the plan to use AppleScript without including the commands
Skill completely omits video metadata handling (title/description) — a core YouTube upload requirement — despite the workflow covering browser automation in detail
Baseline outperforms on structure (5/5 vs 3/5), suggesting the skill's workflow format disrupts natural response organization rather than improving it
strengths: The Chrome profile discovery approach (dynamic listing vs hardcoding) is technically sound and shows domain knowledge
The skill correctly identifies AppleScript as the right tool for macOS file picker automation
actionItems.suggestedFocus: Rewrite the skill to include complete, ready-to-run code blocks for each step (file check, AppleScript dialog handler, title/description prompt) so the LLM executes actions rather than narrating plans.
